rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical halogen-atom transfer (e-Shuttle) that transfers Br2 or Cl2 from a vicinal dihaloalkane donor to an alkene acceptor, regenerating an alkene from the donor and producing a 1,2-dihalide on the acceptor.
electrochemical_role: Paired electrolysis: cathodic 2e- reduction of the vicinal dihaloalkane donor releases 2 X- and the alkene byproduct; the halide is then oxidized back to X+ equivalent at the anode (directly for Br2; via a Mn2+/Mn3+ mediator for Cl2) which halogenates the acceptor alkene.

key_intermediates.name: bromide anion released by cathodic reduction
electrophilic Br+ equivalent generated at anode
Mn3+-Cl (oxidized Mn(III) chloride mediator that delivers Cl to alkene)

electron_flow: At the cathode, the vicinal dihaloalkane donor accepts 2 electrons and fragments to an alkene plus 2 X- (E2-type reductive dehalogenation). For Br2 shuttle, Br- is oxidized at the anode to a Br+ equivalent (bromiranium) that adds across the acceptor alkene to give the 1,2-dibromide; for Cl2 shuttle, Cl- is captured by Mn2+ and the Mn2+-Cl is anodically oxidized to Mn3+-Cl, which transfers Cl2 to the alkene to give the 1,2-dichloride.

probe_reagents.name: 1,2-dibromoethane (donor)
probe_reagents.observation: Used as Br2 donor; absence of HBr elimination products supports a 2e- reductive debromination releasing ethylene rather than HX elimination.

probe_reagents.name: 2,3-dibromobutane (donor)
probe_reagents.observation: Donor releases 2-butene cleanly, again ruling out HX elimination pathway.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1,1,2-tetrachloroethane (donor)
probe_reagents.observation: Acts as Cl2 donor without producing HCl-elimination byproducts.

probe_reagents.name: 1,2-dichloroethane (DCE, donor)
probe_reagents.observation: Serves as Cl2 donor, releasing ethylene; consistent with reductive dechlorination.

probe_reagents.name: Lindane (gamma-hexachlorocyclohexane, donor)
probe_reagents.observation: Stepwise Cl2 transfer ultimately yields benzene, confirmed by crude NMR and GC, supporting sequential reductive dechlorination releasing alkene intermediates.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of Br2 shuttle components
probe_reagents.observation: CV demonstrates the redox couple responsible for Br- oxidation / dibromide reduction supporting the proposed paired electrochemistry.

probe_reagents.name: Representative intramolecular Br2 transfer experiment
probe_reagents.observation: An intramolecular variant confirms net Br2 transfer from donor to acceptor alkene.

notes: Authors explicitly rule out an HX-elimination pathway (Section 6) by showing donors lacking acidic beta-H or known to favor elimination still cleanly release alkenes and transfer X2. The Cl2 cycle requires a Mn2+/Mn3+ mediator at the anode because chloride oxidation is otherwise too anodic, whereas the Br2 cycle proceeds without a metal mediator via direct anodic Br- oxidation. Lindane remediation is demonstrated as a practical application of sequential Cl2 transfer to benzene.
